title: A Volcano That's Been Erupting for 2,000 Years
script: There's a volcano that has been erupting almost nonstop for over two thousand years, and people still live right next to it. It's called Stromboli, a small island off Italy, and it fires glowing chunks of lava into the sky every few minutes like clockwork. Sailors have used its constant glow as a natural lighthouse for centuries, calling it the lighthouse of the Mediterranean. Most eruptions are small, but every so often it lets out a big one that sends ash and rock raining down. The people who live there have simply learned to time their lives around a mountain that never sleeps. So why would anyone build a home on an active volcano?
